name: Ember.js
description: A JavaScript framework for creating ambitious web applications with strong conventions and batteries included

description.value: A JavaScript framework for creating ambitious web applications with strong conventions and batteries included

initial-release.comment: Originally SproutCore 2.0, renamed to Ember.js in December 2011. Created by Yehuda Katz.

active-maintenance.comment: Regular 6-week release cycle; version 6.7 released September 2025

reactivity-model.comment: Autotracking reactivity system since Ember Octane; tracks @tracked properties automatically

component-model.comment: Glimmer components with class-based definition and separate .hbs templates; also supports .gjs/.gts single-file format

state-management-approach.comment: Services provide singleton state management; Ember Data for model layer; no external state library required

opinionatedness.comment: Strong conventions, batteries included philosophy - similar to Ruby on Rails

backward-compatibility.comment: Very strong commitment; apps from 2011 can still run on modern Ember. LTS releases with 36 weeks of bugfixes, 54 weeks of security updates

typescript-support.comment: First-class TypeScript support via ember-cli-typescript and Glint for template type-checking

template-syntax.comment: Handlebars-based templates (.hbs files); also supports .gjs/.gts single-file components with <template> tags

styling-approach.comment: No built-in styling solution; works with CSS, Sass, CSS modules via addons

build-required.comment: Ember CLI build required; not designed for CDN usage

reactive-primitives.comment: @tracked decorator for reactive properties in Glimmer components

computed-values.comment: Native getters auto-track dependencies; @cached decorator for memoization

two-way-binding.comment: Two-way binding available via {{input}} helper and {{mut}} helper

event-handling.comment: {{on}} modifier for event handling in templates

component-slots.comment: {{yield}} for content projection; named blocks for multiple slots

teleport-portal.comment: {{in-element}} helper for rendering outside component tree

suspense.comment: No built-in Suspense equivalent; async patterns handled differently

error-boundaries.comment: No built-in error boundary component; errors typically handled via route error substates

context-provide-inject.comment: Services provide dependency injection pattern; injected via @service decorator

refs-direct-dom-access.comment: {{did-insert}} modifier and element modifiers for DOM access

official-router.comment: Router is a core part of Ember.js, not a separate package

nested-routes.comment: First-class nested routes support with outlet for child route rendering

route-guards.comment: beforeModel, model, afterModel hooks for navigation guards

lazy-loading-routes.comment: Route-based code splitting via Embroider build system

scroll-restoration.comment: Built-in scroll position restoration in router

built-in-global-state.comment: Services are singletons for app-wide state; Ember Data for model state

global-state-library.comment: Ember Data for model layer; Services for app-wide state - both are first-party

devtools-state-inspection.comment: Ember Inspector shows component tree, routes, Ember Data records

state-persistence.comment: No built-in persistence; available via addons like ember-local-storage

time-travel-debugging.comment: Ember Inspector doesn't support time-travel debugging

official-cli.comment: Ember CLI - comprehensive project scaffolding and build tool

hot-module-replacement.comment: Live reload built into Ember CLI development server

browser-devtools.comment: Ember Inspector extension for Chrome and Firefox

vs-code-extension.comment: Official Ember.js Extension Pack with Language Server, Glimmer syntax highlighting

error-messages.comment: Good error messages with component context; Ember Inspector helps debugging

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive guides with tutorials, API docs, and interactive Super Rentals tutorial

interactive-tutorial.comment: Super Rentals tutorial - comprehensive guided project

playground.comment: No official online playground; typically use Ember CLI locally

bundle-size-full.comment: ~120 kB gzipped with Ember Data; varies by app configuration

virtual-dom.comment: Uses Glimmer VM with bytecode compilation, not traditional virtual DOM diffing

compile-time-optimization.comment: Glimmer compiler compiles templates to bytecode; Embroider enables tree-shaking

tree-shaking.comment: Via Embroider build system; traditional Ember builds don't tree-shake

ssr-capable.comment: Via FastBoot addon for server-side rendering

partial-hydration.comment: FastBoot supports rehydration - Glimmer VM reuses server-rendered DOM

js-framework-benchmark.comment: Not consistently included in recent js-framework-benchmark results

component-libraries.comment: ember-power-select, ember-basic-dropdown, Syncfusion Ember, ember-paper (Material), sl-ember-components

meta-framework.comment: Ember is batteries-included; no separate meta-framework needed. FastBoot provides SSR.

major-companies-using.value: LinkedIn, Apple Music, Netflix, Microsoft, Square, Heroku, Intercom, Discourse

official-sponsor-backer.comment: Community-driven; originally created at Tilde Inc. No major corporate sponsor like Meta or Google.

job-market-demand.comment: Niche but stable demand; ~300 jobs on LinkedIn US. Strong at specific companies like LinkedIn.

official-testing-library.comment: ember-qunit comes built-in with every Ember app

testing-library-name.comment: QUnit-based testing with ember-qunit helpers; also supports Mocha via ember-mocha

e2e-testing-integration.comment: Works with Playwright, Cypress; built-in acceptance tests without Selenium

snapshot-testing.comment: Supported via jest-serializer-html-string or similar tools

mobile-framework.comment: ember-cordova and Capacitor integration available; no official mobile framework

pwa-support.comment: Via ember-service-worker addon for PWA capabilities

desktop-framework.comment: ember-electron for Electron; Tauri integration documented

learning-curve.comment: Steep learning curve due to many conventions, concepts, and CLI tooling to learn

concepts-to-learn.comment: Routes, Controllers, Components, Services, Models, Templates, Helpers, Modifiers, Ember Data, Ember CLI, Glimmer, Autotracking

time-to-hello-world.comment: ~10 minutes to generate new app and create first component via Ember CLI

migration-path.comment: No official guides for migrating from other frameworks

incremental-adoption.comment: Ember is designed as a full framework; not meant for incremental adoption in existing apps
